questions.question: After changing the scheduling algorithm from FCFS to Shortest Job First (SJF), the simulator shows a decrease in Average Waiting Time. What is the most likely reason?

answers.a: Shorter jobs are processed first, reducing the waiting time for most jobs.
answers.b: The number of machines automatically increased.

explanations.a: Correct. SJF gives priority to jobs with the shortest processing time, which generally reduces the average waiting time and improves production flow.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Changing the scheduling algorithm does not change the number of machines.
explanations.c: Incorrect. The production queue still exists regardless of the scheduling algorithm.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Machine processing speed remains unchanged unless modified by the user.

questions.question: During the simulation, Machine A remains busy while Machine C stays idle for a long period. What does this indicate?

answers.a: Poor machine allocation leading to unbalanced resource utilization.
answers.b: Production throughput has reached its maximum.
answers.d: All machines are operating with equal utilization.

explanations.a: Correct. Uneven workload distribution causes some machines to be overloaded while others remain underutilized, reducing scheduling efficiency.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Maximum throughput cannot be concluded from one machine remaining idle.
explanations.c: Incorrect. If one machine is continuously busy, production jobs are still being processed.
explanations.d: Incorrect. The scenario clearly indicates unequal machine utilization.

questions.question: The production queue continues to grow even though all machines are active. What is the most appropriate conclusion?

answers.a: The job arrival rate is higher than the production capacity, creating a bottleneck.
answers.b: The scheduling algorithm has stopped functioning.
answers.d: The completed jobs are being deleted.

explanations.a: Correct. When production demand exceeds processing capacity, waiting jobs accumulate, resulting in a production bottleneck.
explanations.b: Incorrect. A growing queue does not necessarily indicate scheduler failure.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Busy machines indicate high utilization rather than zero utilization.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Job completion records do not affect queue formation.

questions.question: Which scheduling algorithm should be selected if urgent customer orders must always be processed before normal orders?

explanations.a: Correct. Priority Scheduling processes jobs according to their assigned priority, ensuring urgent jobs are completed first.
explanations.b: Incorrect. FCFS processes jobs only according to arrival order.
explanations.c: Incorrect. SJF considers processing time rather than urgency.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Round Robin allocates equal processor time and does not prioritize urgent jobs.

questions.question: The simulator reports a decrease in Makespan after increasing the number of active machines. What does this indicate?

answers.a: The total production completion time has been reduced due to improved parallel processing.
answers.c: The production queue has permanently disappeared.
answers.d: The scheduling algorithm is no longer required.

explanations.a: Correct. Additional machines allow more jobs to be processed simultaneously, reducing the overall completion time of the production schedule.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Additional machines do not reduce utilization to zero.
explanations.c: Incorrect. A queue may still exist depending on production demand.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Scheduling remains essential regardless of the number of machines.

questions.question: During the simulation, Machine Utilization increases from 62% to 91% after changing the scheduling algorithm. What is the most appropriate interpretation?

answers.a: The production machines are being utilized more efficiently, resulting in better resource usage.
answers.b: The machines have become physically larger.
answers.c: The production queue has been removed permanently.
answers.d: The processing time of every job has become zero.

explanations.a: Correct. Higher machine utilization indicates that machines spend more time processing jobs instead of remaining idle, leading to improved production efficiency.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Machine utilization measures operating time, not the physical dimensions of a machine.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Queue length depends on production demand and scheduling decisions, not machine utilization alone.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Jobs still require processing time even when machine utilization increases.

questions.question: After increasing the Job Arrival Rate, the simulator shows a significant increase in Queue Length. What is the most likely reason?

answers.a: Production jobs are arriving faster than the machines can process them.
answers.b: Machine processing speed has automatically increased.
answers.c: The scheduling algorithm has stopped assigning jobs.
answers.d: Completed jobs are being processed again.

explanations.a: Correct. When production demand exceeds the processing capacity of available machines, waiting jobs accumulate in the production queue.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Increasing machine processing speed would normally reduce the queue length.
explanations.c: Incorrect. A growing queue does not necessarily indicate scheduler failure.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Completed jobs are removed from the production system and are not processed again.

questions.question: Which production scheduling algorithm is generally expected to produce the lowest Average Waiting Time when job processing times are known in advance?

explanations.a: Correct. SJF processes shorter jobs first, which generally minimizes the average waiting time compared to other scheduling algorithms.
explanations.b: Incorrect. FCFS follows arrival order and may cause long jobs to delay shorter ones.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Priority Scheduling focuses on job importance rather than minimizing waiting time.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Round Robin emphasizes fairness rather than minimizing average waiting time.

questions.question: The simulator reports an increase in Production Throughput after reducing the Average Waiting Time. What does this indicate?

answers.a: More production jobs are being completed within the same period.
answers.b: The production machines have consumed more electrical energy.
answers.c: Machine utilization has decreased to zero.
answers.d: The number of customer orders has automatically reduced.

explanations.a: Correct. Lower waiting time allows jobs to begin processing sooner, increasing the number of completed jobs over time and improving throughput.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Throughput measures production output, not electrical energy consumption.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Increased throughput usually requires machines to remain actively processing jobs.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Customer order quantity is independent of throughput calculations.

questions.question: Based on the simulation results, which production system demonstrates the most effective scheduling strategy?

answers.a: High Machine Utilization, Low Average Waiting Time, Low Queue Length, High Throughput, and Low Makespan
answers.b: Long Waiting Time, High Queue Length, and Low Machine Utilization
answers.c: Frequent Machine Idle Time with Increasing Production Delays
answers.d: High Makespan with Low Production Efficiency

explanations.a: Correct. An efficient scheduling strategy maximizes resource utilization while minimizing waiting time, queue length, and production completion time, resulting in high productivity.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Long waiting times and high queue lengths indicate poor scheduling performance.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Idle machines and increasing delays reduce manufacturing efficiency.
explanations.d: Incorrect. High makespan and low production efficiency are indicators of an ineffective production schedule.
